howItWorks.intro: This allergy severity score calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: When should I see an allergist?
faq.answer: When OTC medications don't control symptoms or allergies impact daily life.

faq.question: What is immunotherapy?
faq.answer: Allergy shots or sublingual tablets that desensitize you to allergens over time.

faq.question: Is the Allergy Severity Assessment Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What sneezing should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate sneezing value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Allergy Severity Assessment Calculator?
sections.body: The Allergy Severity Assessment Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your sneezing, nasal congestion, eye itchiness, you get instant results including allergy severity score, severity level, recommended action. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting allergy severity score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your sneezing and need to find the right allergy severity score. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Sneezing (episodes/day)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your sneezing — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: nasal congestion, eye itchiness, daily life interference. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your allergy severity score, severity level, recommended action. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your sneezing before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both allergy severity score and severity level — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your daily life interference, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
